title: trymalium obovatum
intro: Trymalium obovatum, also known as the Woolly Bush or Woolly Peely, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of wounds. Traditionally, the plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
